title: Talk:Mohammad Pakpour
wikitext:
{{WikiProject banner shell|class=C|listas=Pakpour, Mohammad|blp=no| {{WikiProject Biography|military-work-group=y|military-priority=Low}} {{WikiProject Iran}} {{WikiProject Military history|Biography=y|Middle-Eastern=y|Cold-War=y|class=Start|b1=yes|b2=no|b3=yes|b4=yes|b5=yes}} }} == present at fallujah operation == i think that fact he was present alongside qassem soleimani in fallujah is an obviously important fact, and Long War Journal IS a RELIABLE SOURCE, so i will add it back in. if anyone objects, try actually talking on the talkpage rather than reverting randomly — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/82.112.144.10|82.112.144.10]] ([[User talk:82.112.144.10|talk]]) 16:51, 9 June 2016 (UTC) :Please re-read [[WP:RS]]. Long War Journal has none of the indicia of being a reliable source, as that term is defined on Wikipedia. I'm going to seek the advice of editors from the Reliable Sources Notice Board. [[User:David in DC|David in DC]] ([[User talk:David in DC|talk]]) 19:30, 9 June 2016 (UTC) ::Additional opinions requested [[Wikipedia:Reliable_sources/Noticeboard/Archive_208#Mohammad_Pakpour|here]]. [[User:David in DC|David in DC]] ([[User talk:David in DC|talk]]) 19:41, 9 June 2016 (UTC) :::Responding to the RSN post. Upon review, this does not appear to be a reliable source. That said, we can look up for other sources that support the section. [[User:DaltonCastle|DaltonCastle]] ([[User talk:DaltonCastle|talk]]) 23:08, 9 June 2016 (UTC) :::Just to be clear, I am stating my belief that ''The Long War Journal'' does not pass as a reliable source. [[User:DaltonCastle|DaltonCastle]] ([[User talk:DaltonCastle|talk]]) 23:10, 9 June 2016 (UTC) ::::[[User talk:82.112.144.10|User:82.112.144.10]], please review the comment above, and also another editor's opinion from the Reliable Sources Noticeboard [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Wikipedia%3AReliable_sources%2FNoticeboard&type=revision&diff=724538097&oldid=724518769]. I think [[User:DaltonCastle|DaltonCastle]] has a good suggestion above. If the matter is truly notable, we should be able to find it in another source, one that complies with [[WP:RS]]. I'll start looking. I hope you will too. [[User:David in DC|David in DC]] ([[User talk:David in DC|talk]]) 13:54, 10 June 2016 (UTC) I replied the following on the noticeboard but it was archived, maybe before you saw it:"the Long War Journal" is not merely a "blog" or "website", it is an an ACADEMIC PROJECT of the Foundation for Defense of Democracies (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foundation_for_Defense_of_Democracies), a respected Washington DC think-tank. The Wiki page for the project (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Long_War_Journal) itself states: The Long War Journal has been used as a SOURCE by a number of large, mass media organizations. So really, yes, it is a reliable source and can be used to put the info on the Pakpour page. it is quite clearly reliable source so I am going to put it back — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/82.112.144.10|82.112.144.10]] ([[User talk:82.112.144.10|talk]]) 15:45, 27 June 2016 (UTC) ::I have reverted the re-insertion of this contested information. Under [[WP:BRD]], it's not sufficient for an editor to assert that he/she is right. The editor must convince other editors, thereby creating a new consensus. That has not happened. Please edit in compliance with our rules.
The archived Reliable Sources Noticeboard thread does not show a consensus about LWJ being a [[WP:RS|reliable source]] either. I get that this is obvious to you. But, again, that's not enough. Others must be persuaded. That has not happened. [[User:David in DC|David in DC]] ([[User talk:David in DC|talk]]) 18:23, 27 June 2016 (UTC) you've written this, but you haven't bothered to give your own opinion here. and you didn't just remove the contested bit, you removed all the new reliably sourced stuff i added regarded the west iran clashes why don't you try following the rules, so heres a rule for you: IGNORE ALL RULES >If a rule prevents you from improving or maintaining Wikipedia, IGNORE IT. OR how about, WIKIPEDIA IS NOT A BUREAUCRACY! https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:What_Wikipedia_is_not#Wikipedia_is_not_a_bureaucracy OR MAYBE, USE COMMON SENSE? https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:What_%22Ignore_all_rules%22_means#Use_common_sense You are coming back to this page and deliberately, for whatever reason, damaging the page rather than accepting my improvements over your obviously incorrect, contrary to common sense assertion that LWJ violates the rules on reliable source. You are not the BUREAUCRACY of Wikipedia, and I am ignoring your damned rules. I will keep reverting my good content. — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/82.112.144.10|82.112.144.10]] ([[User talk:82.112.144.10|talk]]) 23:03, 27 June 2016 (UTC) == Pakpour does not command the IRGC Ground Forces anymore == Mohammad Pakpour -> Mohammad Karami [[Special:Contributions/87.95.84.19|87.95.84.19]] ([[User talk:87.95.84.19|talk]]) 07:51, 24 June 2025 (UTC) == Death == is he dead [[Special:Contributions/~2026-13161-46|~2026-13161-46]] ([[User talk:~2026-13161-46|talk]]) 20:18, 28 February 2026 (UTC) :Yes [[User:ElijahUHC|'''ElijahUHC''']] [[User talk:ElijahUHC|(Talk)]] 20:42, 28 February 2026 (UTC) == How does Wikipedia BLP actually work? == There is a massive edit war going on in Ali Khamanei's page, where the dispute is essentially over current death reports not coming from Iranian/independent sources but rather Israeli/US. And yet this page (and that of Aziz Nasirzadeh) already list them as dead, even though the sourcing is again solely from Israeli authorities. Do you guys just make shit up as you go? [[Special:Contributions/~2026-13208-53|~2026-13208-53]] ([[User talk:~2026-13208-53|talk]]) 22:08, 28 February 2026 (UTC) :Yes, they do. They have let Wiki become a leftist mess. [[Special:Contributions/~2026-13203-57|~2026-13203-57]] ([[User talk:~2026-13203-57|talk]]) 03:18, 1 March 2026 (UTC) ::absolute goymaxxing over here [[User:Ijustlikefootball|Ijustlikefootball]] ([[User talk:Ijustlikefootball|talk]]) 22:28, 1 March 2026 (UTC)

sections.line: Pakpour does not command the IRGC Ground Forces anymore
How does Wikipedia BLP actually work?
